provisions.title: APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS
provisions.content:

      MILITARY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DOMINGO.
                Executive Order No. 811.
              APPROPRIATION OF FUNDS.
                                                      -
                                                      1

    By virtue of the powers vested in the Military Government
of Santo Domingo, the following Executive Order is hereby pro-
mulgated :
     ARTICLE 1. Thei-e is hereby appropriated froim funds of
the National Treasury the sum of FOUR THOUSAND DOLLARS
 ($4,000) f o r defraying expenses incidient to a complete audit
by an agent of the Military Government of the acclounts of the
Fiscal Agent of the Dominican Repubilic, The Guaranty Trust
Company, in connection with the $20,000,000.00 Cuistomis Ad-
ministration Eonld Issue of 1908, which audit shall be f o r the
period from t h e time these bonds were originally issued to Sep-
tember 30, 1922.

                                   S . S. ROBIWN,
                           Fbar A,drnimd, United S.tat,es Navy,

Sa;nto Dlomingo, R. D.,
October 14. 1922.

t h e sum of five hundred dollars ($500), for t h e aid of those wh@

Santo Domingo, R. D.,

      MILITARY GOVERNMENT OF SANTO DOMINGO.
                     Executive Order N o . 813.
               APPOINTING SPECIAL REFEREE.

     ITHEREAS, the Department of Sanitation and Beneficence
occupied the premises known as No. 17 Calle Hostos during the
priucl from June 11, 1920 t o January 1, 1922, in accordmce with
an agreement entersd into on June 8, 1920, between the owner
thereof and the then Officer in Charge of the Department of Sani-
tation and Beneficence ; and
      KINEREAS, for purposes of economy, t h e Department of
Sanitation and Beneficence vacated the said premises oln January
1, 1922, although the period mentioned in t h e said agreement
h a d not expired ; and
     WHEREAAS, by decision dated March 31, 1922 the Auditor
of the Dominican Republic, acting under the authority of the L2y
de Haciewda, Executive Order No. 563, as amendled, rejected in
